get.description:
Returns the login methods supported for a given mobile number.

The response always includes `otp`. If the user has a password set,
`password` is also included. The frontend should prefer showing the
password form when both are available, as it provides a faster and
more familiar experience for returning users.

Use this endpoint to build a dynamic login screen:
- if only `otp` → show OTP input
- if both → show password + fallback to OTP

post.description:
Sends a One‑Time Password (OTP) to the provided mobile number.

This is the first step in the OTP‑based login flow.
The system applies rate limiting (resend delay) and anti‑abuse mechanisms
(temporary bans if too many OTPs are requested without verification).

> In local environment, the OTP code is returned in the response for testing.

Authenticates a user using either a password or an OTP verification code.

The user must provide a mobile number and exactly one of the two credentials:
- `password` (if the user has a password set)
- `verification_code` (if using OTP flow)

Upon successful authentication, the response sets HTTP‑only cookies for
access and refresh tokens. The `Set-Cookie` headers are included.

Set-Cookie.description: Set access token and refresh token

schema.example: SameSite=Lax access_token=eyJ...; expires=Fri, 19 Jun 2026 15:22:19 GMT; Max-Age=957; path=/; domain=sample.com; secure; httponly; samesite=strict refresh_token=eyJ...; expires=Fri, 26 Jun 2026 15:07:19 GMT; Max-Age=604857; path=/; domain=sample.com; secure; httponly; samesite=strict

post.description:
Refreshes the access token using the refresh token stored in a cookie.

This endpoint is used to obtain a new access token without re‑entering
credentials. The refresh token must be present in the `refresh_token`
cookie. On success, new access and refresh tokens are set in cookies.

If the refresh token is invalid or expired, the response clears the
cookies and returns an error.

Set-Cookie.description: Set access token and refresh token

schema.example: SameSite=Lax access_token=eyJ...; expires=Fri, 19 Jun 2026 15:22:19 GMT; Max-Age=957; path=/; domain=sample.com; secure; httponly; samesite=strict refresh_token=eyJ...; expires=Fri, 26 Jun 2026 15:07:19 GMT; Max-Age=604857; path=/; domain=sample.com; secure; httponly; samesite=strict

Set-Cookie.description: Forget access token and refresh token

schema.example: SameSite=Lax access_token=deleted; expires=Thu, 01 Jan 1970 00:00:01 GMT; Max-Age=0; path=/; domain=sample.com; secure; httponly; samesite=strict refresh_token=deleted; expires=Thu, 01 Jan 1970 00:00:01 GMT; Max-Age=0; path=/; domain=sample.com; secure; httponly; samesite=strict

Set-Cookie.description: Forget access token and refresh token

schema.example: SameSite=Lax access_token=deleted; expires=Thu, 01 Jan 1970 00:00:01 GMT; Max-Age=0; path=/; domain=sample.com; secure; httponly; samesite=strict refresh_token=deleted; expires=Thu, 01 Jan 1970 00:00:01 GMT; Max-Age=0; path=/; domain=sample.com; secure; httponly; samesite=strict

delete.description:
Logs out the currently authenticated user.

Invalidates the access token and clears the authentication cookies
(`access_token` and `refresh_token`). After logout, the client must
re-authenticate to access protected endpoints.



> **Note:**
> The user must be authenticated to use this endpoint.

Set-Cookie.description: Forget access token and refresh token

schema.example: SameSite=Lax access_token=deleted; expires=Thu, 01 Jan 1970 00:00:01 GMT; Max-Age=0; path=/; domain=sample.com; secure; httponly; samesite=strict refresh_token=deleted; expires=Thu, 01 Jan 1970 00:00:01 GMT; Max-Age=0; path=/; domain=sample.com; secure; httponly; samesite=strict

delete.__note:
> **Note:**
> The user must be authenticated to use this endpoint.

get.description:
Verifies whether the current user is authenticated.

This endpoint is used to validate the session and access token validity.
It returns a simple boolean response indicating if the user is currently
authenticated. Useful for client-side checks before making other requests.



> **Note:**
> The user must be authenticated to use this endpoint.
get.__note:
> **Note:**
> The user must be authenticated to use this endpoint.

post.description:
Creates a new temporary volume for file uploads.

The volume is associated with the authenticated user and a specific
collection (`place`). Its terms (capacity, file constraints) are defined
by the collection configuration. The volume has a time‑to‑live (`TTL`)
after which it expires and cannot be used.

Use this before uploading files to obtain a volume **`UUID`**.

get.description:
Fetches details of a specific volume.

Returns the volume metadata (UUID, place, expiration time) and the list
of objects (files) it contains. Each object includes its UUID and file
details (URL, size, MIME type, etc.).

Retrieves the terms and constraints for a given collection (place).

Returns information such as:
- capacity (maximum number of objects)
- allowed file size range (min/max in KB)
- allowed extensions
- allowed MIME types
- allowed image dimensions (if applicable)

This is useful for client‑side validation before uploading files.

post.description:
Uploads a file to an existing volume.

The file must comply with the volume's terms (size, extensions, MIME types,
image dimensions). The volume must not be expired and must have available
capacity. On success, the uploaded file is added as a new object in the volume.

This endpoint is used after creating a volume and retrieving its terms.

delete.description:
Removes a specific file object from a volume.

The volume must be owned by the authenticated user and not expired.
Deletion is permanent; the file is removed from storage.
After deletion, the object's **`UUID`** becomes invalid.

fields.description: Fields may be absent, or their values may match one or more predefined messages under different conditions.

example: The mobile field is required.
The mobile must be a valid 11-digit mobile number starting with 09.
otp
password

fields.description: Fields may be absent, or their values may match one or more predefined messages under different conditions.

example: The mobile field is required.
The mobile must be a valid 11-digit mobile number starting with 09.
The password field is required when verification code is not present.
The password field prohibits verification code from being present.
The password must be a string.
The password must not be greater than 1024 characters.
The verification code field is required when password is not present.
The verification code field prohibits password from being present.
The verification code must not be greater than 16 characters.

LoginRequest.description: Login credentials. Exactly one of password or verification_code must be supplied.

password.description: User password (required if verification_code is absent).

verification_code.description: OTP verification code (required if password is absent).

resend_in.description: Second remaining to system allows same mobile number to receive another OTP.

code.description: The one time password that has been sent to mobile

cooldown.description: Number of seconds the client must wait before making another OTP request.
Time (in seconds) until the temporary ban is lifted and OTP requests are allowed again.

message.const: This recipient has been temporarily banned due to suspicious activity.

fields.description: Fields may be absent, or their values may match one or more predefined messages under different conditions.

example: The file field is required.
The file must be a file.
The file must be at least <MIN> kilobytes.
The file must not be greater than <MAX> kilobytes.
The file must have one of the following extensions: <EXTEXTENSION>, <EXTENSION>.
The file must be a file of type: <MIME>, <MIME>.
The file dimension is invalid.

fields.description: Fields may be absent, or their values may match one or more predefined messages under different conditions.

example: The place field is required.
The place field must be a string.
The place field must not be greater than 128 characters.
The selected place is invalid.

message.const: The client does not have access rights to the content.
Authenticated users cannot access this resource.
The server encountered an unexpected condition.
The server cannot find the requested resource.
Authentication is required and has failed or not been provided.

is_bio_completed.description: Indicates whether the user's bio information (first_name, last_name, birth_date, gender) is completed.

is_ssn_verified.description: Indicates whether the user's SSN (national identification number) has been verified and is still valid (not expired).

DateTime.description:
RFC 3339 timestamp (a subset of ISO 8601).

Represents a date and time with an explicit time‑zone offset from UTC.

> Format: `YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm:ss±HH:mm` or `YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm:ssZ` (UTC).
> Fractions of a second are allowed but not required for this implementation.

meta.description: Additional metadata – structure varies depending on the MIME type.

Mobile.description:
Iranian national mobile number (domestic format).

Part of the `ITU-T E.164` framework, but uses the national prefix '0' instead of '+98'.

> Must be 11 digits starting with '09'.

URL.description:
Uniform Resource Locator (URL) as defined by RFC 3986.

A URL is a subset of URI that identifies a network-accessible resource.
The standard syntax is: scheme:[//authority]path[?query][#fragment]

> This implementation follows **RFC 3986** (the current URI generic syntax standard,
> published in 2005, which obsoletes RFC 1738, RFC 2396, etc.).
> For web‑oriented usage, the WHATWG URL Standard is also widely adopted.

externalDocs.description: RFC 3986 – Uniform Resource Identifier (URI): Generic Syntax

UUID.description:
Universally Unique Identifier (UUID) as defined by RFC 4122.

Represented as a 36‑character string in the canonical 8‑4‑4‑4‑12 hexadecimal form.

> This implementation follows the **RFC 4122** specification and is case‑insensitive.
> Although version 4 (random) is most common, the format is valid for any variant.

example: jpg
png
webp
image/jpg
image/png
image/webp

image_dimensions.description: List of allowed image dimensions (in pixels)

success.description: Indicates whether the request was successful

cookieAuth.description: Access token stored in a cookie (automatically sent by the browser).
cookieAuth.name: access_token
